About

Author bio, methodology, data sources, related publications, and external profiles.

Bio

Author
Marcin Dec, PhD
Assistant Professor in Finance, Department of Finance, Kozminski University, Warsaw.
PhD in Quantitative Economics, Collegium of Economic Analysis, SGH Warsaw School of Economics. Research Assistant at FAME|GRAPE since 2019. Twenty-plus years of fixed-income practitioner experience as senior analyst, portfolio manager, and risk manager before entering academia.
Research focus
Yield-curve modelling for less-liquid sovereign bond markets, term-premium estimation, multi-curve HJM, money-market benchmarks, valuation adjustments (XVA), and welfare measurement with heterogeneous agents. Empirical anchor is the Polish PLN sovereign curve, with comparative work against US Treasury and euro-area AAA benchmarks.
Funding
National Science Centre Poland, Preludium grant UMO-2020/37/N/HS4/02202 (2021–2024). Multiple SGH Rector's scholarships for best doctoral students, 2017–2021.
This site
yieldcartography.com publishes the daily-refreshed empirical outputs of the research programme: NSS-fitted zero-coupon yield curves, ACM and BRW term-premium decompositions, microstructure-based liquidity measures, and expectations-hypothesis test results. All code is open, all data is publicly sourced, and the underlying CSVs are linked below.

Selected publications

  • Welfare measurements with heterogeneous agents with Marek Weretka — Journal of Economic Dynamics and Control, 2025
  • Yield Curve Modelling with the Nelson-Siegel Method for Poland Gospodarka Narodowa, 2/2022, 44–56
  • Forecasting the Yield Curve for Poland Econometric Research in Finance, 5(2), 103–117, 2020
  • Markovian and multi-curve friendly parametrisation of HJM model used in valuation adjustment of interest rate derivatives working paper
  • Parsimonious yield curve modeling in less liquid markets FAME|GRAPE working paper
  • Extracting Risk-Free Interest Rate Expectations in Less Liquid Government Bond Markets FAME|GRAPE working paper

For the full publication list and ongoing working papers see the GRAPE and RePEc profiles linked above.

Data sources

Polish bond panel: BondSpot venue closing fixings (price, bid, ask, turnover) — aggregated from Polish Ministry of Finance disclosures.
Outstanding amounts and turnover weights: Polish Min-Fin monthly statistics; pre-2014 panel reconstructed from KDPW MD pivots.
Short-end anchor: NBP money-market bill rate.
NBP Survey of Professional Forecasters: 57 quarterly survey releases, implied short-rate path constructed per PhD Chapter 5.4 methodology.
US Treasury zeros: Gürkaynak-Sack-Wright (GSW) updated panel.
Euro-area zeros: ECB AAA spot-rate database.
Macro panel: CPI, unemployment, FX volatility, NBP rate changes.

Methodology in one paragraph

The Polish zero-coupon curve is fitted with a liquidity-weighted Nelson-Siegel-Svensson specification (LW-NSS), where bond-level weights are derived from prior-month BondSpot turnover and outstanding amounts. The fitter enforces standard sign and ordering constraints on β₀, β₀+β₁, τ₁ and τ₂, applies a τ₁ floor of 1.0 to suppress short-end curvature artefacts, and uses a small β₂/β₃ ridge penalty to stabilise the curvature factors. Term premia are computed via the Adrian-Crump-Moench (2013) affine term-structure model on five Nelson-Siegel principal components, with the Bauer-Rudebusch-Wu small-sample bias correction (iterated bootstrap with common random numbers and Kilian shrinkage) as the robustness benchmark. Liquidity measures follow the standard microstructure literature: bid-ask spreads, zero-trading-day frequency, Amihud (2002) illiquidity, Roll (1984), Pastor-Stambaugh γ, Corwin-Schultz (2012) high-low spread, and a composite z-index. Expectations-hypothesis tests follow the Fama-Bliss FB1/FB2 framework, the Cochrane-Piazzesi single and seagull factor regressions, an out-of-sample STV exercise against a random walk, and the cross-market PL/US/EA pooling robustness.
